speaker: Archivist
description: Voice-reference dialogue samples for the Archivist NPC in Pig & Swine RPG. These are draft samples, not final committed game text.
source_role_summary: Gatekeeper for old files, procedural binders, notices, registers, drafts, maps, and municipal records
Chapter 1 source for the Procedural Binder and service-history clue
Chapter 2 source for old building rules, prior notices, and comparator displays
Chapter 3 source for filing register anomalies and print-room / courier records
Chapter 4 source for old contract drafts, travel records, and arbitration bundle discipline
Chapter 5 source for notice-template history, redevelopment records, objection-register traces, and final proof trail

voice_profile.core_voice: dry, precise, territorial about records, quietly kind to serious researchers, allergic to loose paper, formal but with buried warmth
comedy_engine: treats files as living creatures with rights to correct shelving
speaks of chaos as archival violence
rewards careful labels and punishes vague requests
turns grand legal drama into accession numbers, registers, tabs, and dust
is funny because their extreme orderliness is often the only thing saving Pig & Swine
do: make the Archivist useful but demanding
make archival precision feel like gameplay
give hints through records, dates, versions, registers, and missing file trails
keep them dry rather than whimsical
show quiet respect for Asia, Murrow, Crab, and anyone who labels things properly
do_not: make them solve the case directly
turn archives into random lore dumps

relationship_dynamics.Dr. A. Cula: initially treats him as a dangerous source of loose paper; gradually trusts him if he asks precise questions
relationship_dynamics.Asia: deep respect; considers her one of the few living people who understands tabs
relationship_dynamics.Mr. Pig: dislikes his shouting near brittle records but tolerates crisis if filed under urgency
relationship_dynamics.Mr. Murrow: respects his structured requests and quiet archival manners
relationship_dynamics.Crab: appreciates his suspicion of dates, signatures, and duplicated lines
relationship_dynamics.Whimsy: tolerates him only when metaphors are stapled to an exhibit
relationship_dynamics.Waldek: sympathetic but insists home-impact evidence must be indexed
relationship_dynamics.Kowalski: finds his notebooks excessive but occasionally valuable
relationship_dynamics.Zielinska: trusts her practical memory when matched with archived records
relationship_dynamics.Bajtek: sees him as a new kind of archivist for machines
relationship_dynamics.Mr. Swine: considers him a walking threat to record integrity
relationship_dynamics.Advocate Szpon: distrusts polished filings that arrive too clean
relationship_dynamics.Court Clerk: professional kinship based on stamps, registers, and hatred of loose annexes
relationship_dynamics.Old Woman: respects her as an oral archive with crumbs
relationship_dynamics.Barista: uses café notice-wall gossip as informal index, with caution

notes: Every line is a voice-reference draft for later rewriting into godot/data/dialogues/dialogues.json.
